Key Takeaways:
o It’s time to start digging into how to build early buzz
o The biggest benefit of the Shot Across the Bow is building buzz
o Building Early Buzz
o Some characteristics of buzz
▪ Remember that every market is different
▪ Buzz travels most easily through channels that have trust and authenticity
▪ It’s great to use multiple modalities and media - email, social media, etc.
▪ A little mystery goes a long way - drop bread crumbs (clues) everywhere
o You want to get the attention of mavens, connectors (as Malcolm Gladwell calls
them), and persons of influence
o It’s good to leave breadcrumbs all around the internet and your network
▪ It’s as if you’re leaving clues to a mystery - people begin wondering what’s
coming
▪ People love to solve mysteries and be insiders - so telling some people
what’s going on makes them feel involved
o Example: PLF 3.0 launch
▪ 6-7 weeks before launch I sent an email to JV’s to give them heads up on
3.0 and get them excited and ready to build buzz
o Example: PLF 3.0 call for case studies
▪ I sent out a call for case studies a few weeks before the launch to PLF
Owners

▪ Sent them to a link to share their story and case study - even allowed
people that aren’t owners, but used free content to create a successful
launch
o This is also a way to let people know something is coming without selling

4
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Now I want to talk about building buzz.
This is getting people excited and talking about what you are going to be doing
even before you go into Prelaunch.
I just showed you about identifying key areas and I have talked about the shot
across the bow and how I love to do that.
One of the things with the shot across the bow is how it gives you good feedback.
You can survey the list, get good feedback.
You can even do the testimonial crunch and get some great testimonials ahead of
time but the real important deal with the shot across the bow is that it lets people know
something is coming before you are actually in Prelaunch, before, when there is no hint
of a pitch, you tell people something is coming and you are excited about it.
If you go back and look at the email that I wrote, it's basically saying, we've been
working on this thing for years, we are so excited, can you just help us out by taking this
survey. It wasn't like hey, we've been working on this thing for years, boy, we are really
excited and please buy it, buy it, buy it.
The big thing with the shot across the bow is you get great feedback, you can do
the testimonial cruncher but the biggest value, I think, is in generating some buzz.
Let's just talk a little bit about the characteristics of buzz.
Every market is different. In some markets, they are more email centric, some
markets might be more forum centric, some might be more live event centric or social
media or whatever. Every market is different.
I mentioned this in the introductory video, the introductory Prelaunch video that
the internet marketing world where I currently sell product, one of the markets that I sell
product into, is very, very connected. Everyone is on everyone's list. People go to forums.
People all do social media. They are all on Twitter or Facebook. It's a super connected
market so I need to hopefully take advantage of that but take that into account when I
am doing my Prelaunch.
Buzz travels most easily through channels that have trust and authenticity.
People don't go and start talking about you and doing word of mouth for you if they think
you are just advertising because they think you are just selling something. The buzz
about something is when people are genuinely excited about it.
What I'm talking about here is like a lot of social media, a lot of forums. You have
to be authentic. The more authentic you are and the more you communicate in these
authentic channels that have trust, the quicker the buzz will spread.
I like to hit multiple modalities so I like to let people know something is coming,
maybe I am interviewed somewhere, maybe I drop a hint in an email, maybe I do a lot of
social media, but I like to use multiple modalities in media.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 7 --

5
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
A little mystery really does go a long way. I will structure it so I'm dropping bread
crumbs here and there and people can try to piece it together. It's almost like a scavenger
hunt. You let some people know something is coming in one area and then you start
talking a little bit about the product in another area. People will feel great about
connecting those two things or those three things and now they've got a secret, right?
The thing about a secret is no one likes to keep a secret. If you can string the
breadcrumbs together and have a little mystery, it can actually go a long way towards
getting the buzz moving along.
I like to think about mavens and connectors. Malcolm Gladwell talked about
them in his book The Tipping Point. Mavens are people that just love to collect data and
information about a whatever, a topic. Connectors are people that know lots and lots of
people, especially lots of people that move in different arenas. A lot of the people you see
on social media, they have very big followings, are mavens and connectors.
Think about persons of influence within your market or niche and those are the
people that I want to reach and get them talking. A person of influence these days
is mostly a publisher. Someone who might publish an email or in a blog or
in social media or wherever. Always think about your champions and how you can
get them involved in your Pre-Prelaunch.
I just mentioned leaving breadcrumbs, leaving clues to a mystery. People love
that. It carries information, they just want to tell other people about it. Let's look at
some examples.
One of the things I'm lucky enough to do is count on my joint venture partners
and my affiliates to create buzz.
It's almost like they are paid buzz creators because what they will do is they will,
when I've got a new version of Product Launch Formula coming out, they will go out into
the market and they will start blogging about it and they will start putting up links about
it.
They may even mention it in social media. They will try to get people excited. Of
course, they are looking for an affiliate commission so one of the things I did with the
Product Launch Formula 3 is start this sort of JV buzz campaign way in advance.
This is an email I sent to my JV's a month before I started Prelaunch so we are
talking probably six or seven weeks before we actually opened our current launch and
I'm just letting them know something is coming, just a quick heads up.
“I've got an all new version of Product Launch Formula coming soon. This will be
Product Launch Formula 3” and then I'll just go on to give them some details. Prelaunch
starts, launch starts here. No trickery, no crazy exciting ninja trick here. I'm just letting
my partners know so they will start blogging. They will start getting the word out if I give
them a heads up.
I put this out a month ahead of time and then I send another one a week later,
another one a week later, so it's every week and then coming down into the final week, I
sent them three or four emails so by the time we went into Prelaunch, I probably sent
them five maybe six emails to start getting them pumped up and then a lot of them are
persons of influence or they are mavens or they are connectors.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
They've got social media followings. They've got their publishers. They've got
email lists and so they are my paid buzz creators and I just want to get them going as far
ahead of time so I can try to amp them up.
You've probably already been through this with me a few times so you know that
it means hundreds of thousands of dollars in commissions just speaking to them, trying
to get them excited.
My last nine launches have done at least a million dollars and this launch will
make it ten in a row so just coming out with some real authority just to sort of convince
them to jump on board and start the buzz creation.
The other example I want to give is also Product Launch Formula 3. A few weeks
ahead of time, I sent this out to my Product Launch Formula Owners and it's a call for
case studies.
It just says okay, “I'm going to collect some case studies for Product Launch
Formula. If you've done a launch and would like to be featured in one of my case studies,
then I'd like to hear from you.”
“You know, I've got PLF3 coming up. I'm going to release an all new Product
Launch Formula in June, PLF3, and it's going to be 100% new from the ground up so I'm
now putting this out there to my list.”
I actually sent this to both my Product Launch Formula Owners and to my
prospect list so 100,000 people are getting this email. It's not my entire market and it's
not like boom, boom, boom, it's Prelaunch, come and get excited about buying my
product.
Here, I'm just coming out and I'm wanting to do case studies and boom, it's a
shot across the bow.
“So many people have done great launches just by studying the free material I
publish. I will even consider people who are not Product Launch Formula Owners”
...here, I'm basically just telling people how great my free content is. People can do
launches just from the free material I publish and it's very much true. I actually have
done case studies with people that have just studied my free stuff.
“Important. Your launch doesn't have to be huge, just one that you will be happy
to share. I've done a case study of a $2000 launch and it was great.”
This is a way ahead of time, assuring people that Product Launch Formula isn't
just for people doing huge big launches.
I put this out and I sent them to a survey, which gathered the data to give me case
studies. Of course, I wanted those case studies to use primarily in my teaching within
Product Launch Formula. In fact, you've already seen the tennis instructional material
and the shot across the bow and that was one of the case studies I got from this query.
I wanted to get case studies, but a big piece of this was the shot across the bow to
let people know that this is coming without coming out and saying hey, I've got
something coming, you should get excited about buying it. This is the shot across the
bow.

© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Between the JV buzz creation that was going on and sending this out to my lists
of which some folks are real fans, some of them are raving fans, and they will go out and
start Twittering about it and putting it on Facebook and talking about it in forums. This
started several weeks before I even went into Prelaunch. I could tell the buzz was
building.
One of the things I do is monitor Twitter for people that mention me by my name
or for Product Launch Formula and I'm monitoring some of the popular forums in my
area and all of a sudden, in the month before Prelaunch, I got people already talking
about me and Product Launch Formula 3.0, my upcoming launch.
A couple other things I did was be interviewed a couple times for popular
membership sites in that month before my launch. I made myself more accessible to be
interviewed.
Normally I don't take too many speaking gigs but I went out and I spoke at a big
event with 400 or 500 attendees. It put me in front of a good audience, just about a week
and a half before Prelaunch, and the thing is, when you are at an event, those people are
often persons of influence.
People that travel to events are often connectors and persons of influence so by
putting myself at an event with 400 or 500 attendees, many of them persons of
influence, by doing a couple of interviews, and by getting my JV partners going, and by
getting my lists going with that shot across the bow, all of a sudden, even weeks before
Prelaunch, I had people anticipating what was coming.
